rows.speaker: A
rows.english: I am leaving, are you going with me ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: nim tangi kuuda nim jote bartaalaa ?
rows.english: Is your younger sister also coming with you ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: When are you coming back from the drama ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: I will come at 10 o'clock. Are you coming ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: illa, nange tumba sustu ; naanu malagtiini, beekaadre naale bartiini.
rows.english: No, I am very much tired ; I will sleep now. If you want, I will come tomorrow.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: nim afiisu estu gante vargee ide ?
rows.english: Upto what time does your office work ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: beligge hattu ganteyinda sanje aidu gantegevarege ide.
rows.english: It works from ten O'clock in the morning to five in the evening.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: dinaa aidu gantege afiisinda manege bartiiraa ?
rows.english: Do you come home from the office at five O'clock everyday ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: heccin dina aidu gantege bartiini, ondond dina maatra aaru gantege barthiini.
rows.english: Most of the days I come at five O'clock. Occassionally I return at six O'clock.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Why i Will there be much work in the office ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, there is too much work in the office.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: iddare, nam ibbariguu tumba kelsa iratte.
rows.english: There is a typist. We both will have too much work.
